data.narrative:
Cambridge Aerospace is a Series A defense and security technology company that has raised $136M to date and is currently valued at approximately $1B. The valuation assessment draws from a comparable universe of 10 private and 2 public companies operating in the same sector and stage, including peers such as Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, and Ares Industries.

Two triangulation methods apply to this valuation. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, yielding $272M based on total raised as a proxy baseline. Peer-median analysis across three same-sector, same-stage companies—Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Ares Industries ($150M)—produces a median valuation of $300M, with a range of $150M to $500M. These methods triangulate to a low estimate of $231M, a median of $286M, and a high of $345M.

The current $1B valuation sits significantly above both analytical methods. Key uncertainties include revenue run-rate and unit economics not provided in this dataset, the degree to which Cambridge's founding team composition and prior defense experience command a premium relative to peers, and the specific terms and dilution structure of the pending $200M Series B round. Resolution of these factors will be material to assessing whether the current valuation reflects defensible defensibility advantages or represents stretch pricing relative to the peer median.
